Title: Create a new user account
Preconditions: User has an Android device with UniMove installed and a valid email address.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., testuser@unive.it), strong password (e.g., P@ssw0rd!), full name (e.g., John Doe)
Test Steps:
1. Open UniMove application.
2. Navigate to the account creation screen.
3. Enter valid email address in the email field.
4. Enter a strong password in the password field.
5. Re-enter the same password in the confirm password field.
6. Enter full name in the name field.
7. Click on the "Create Account" button.
Expected Result: Account is successfully created, and the user is redirected to the login screen with a confirmation message.

Title: Login with valid credentials
Preconditions: User has an existing account in UniMove.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., testuser@unive.it), correct password (e.g., P@ssw0rd!)
Test Steps:
1. Open UniMove application.
2. Navigate to the login screen.
3. Enter valid email address in the email field.
4. Enter correct password in the password field.
5. Click on the "Login" button.
Expected Result: User is successfully logged in and redirected to the home screen.

Title: Insert weekly university schedule and location
Preconditions: User is logged in to UniMove.
Test Data: Valid university schedule (e.g., Monday: 9:00-13:00, Wednesday: 14:00-18:00), valid location (e.g., Mestre)
Test Steps:
1. Open UniMove application.
2. Navigate to the schedule insertion screen.
3. Select the day of the week (e.g., Monday).
4. Enter start time (e.g., 9:00).
5. Enter end time (e.g., 13:00).
6. Select the university location (e.g., Mestre).
7. Repeat steps 3-6 for additional days.
8. Click on the "Save Schedule" button.
Expected Result: Schedule is successfully saved, and the user receives a confirmation message.

Title: Select and save preferred transportation methods
Preconditions: User is logged in to UniMove and has inserted a valid schedule.
Test Data: Preferred transportation methods (e.g., Bus Line 10, Train Line 1)
Test Steps:
1. Open UniMove application.
2. Navigate to the transportation selection screen.
3. Select preferred transportation methods (e.g., Bus Line 10).
4. Click on the "Save Preferences" button.
Expected Result: Selected transportation methods are saved to the user’s profile, and the user receives a confirmation message.

Title: Receive notification for transport disruptions
Preconditions: User is logged in to UniMove, has saved transportation preferences, and has enabled notifications.
Test Data: Simulated transport disruption (e.g., Bus Line 10 is out of service)
Test Steps:
1. Simulate a transport disruption (e.g., Bus Line 10 is out of service).
2. Ensure the user has selected Bus Line 10 as a preferred transportation method.
3. Wait for the system to detect the disruption.
Expected Result: User receives a notification alerting them about the disruption of Bus Line 10, including alternative suggestions if available.

Title: Report an error in travel time estimates
Preconditions: User is logged in to UniMove and has traveled using a transportation method.
Test Data: Error description (e.g., "Bus Line 10 took 30 minutes longer than estimated"), actual travel time (e.g., 45 minutes), estimated travel time (e.g., 15 minutes)
Test Steps:
1. Open UniMove application.
2. Navigate to the error reporting screen.
3. Select the transportation method used (e.g., Bus Line 10).
4. Enter the actual travel time (e.g., 45 minutes).
5. Enter the estimated travel time (e.g., 15 minutes).
6. Enter a description of the error (e.g., "Bus Line 10 took 30 minutes longer than estimated").
7. Click on the "Submit Report" button.
Expected Result: Error report is successfully submitted, and the user receives a confirmation message.

Title: View and edit user profile
Preconditions: User is logged in to UniMove.
Test Data: Updated profile information (e.g., new phone number, updated address)
Test Steps:
1. Open UniMove application.
2. Navigate to the profile screen.
3. Verify displayed information (e.g., name, email, saved transportation methods).
4. Click on the "Edit Profile" button.
5. Update profile information (e.g., new phone number).
6. Click on the "Save Changes" button.
Expected Result: Profile information is successfully updated, and the user receives a confirmation message. Updated information is displayed correctly on the profile screen.

Title: Successful Account Creation with Email Verification
Preconditions: User has installed the app on their device.
Test Data: Valid email address, strong password, confirmed password
Test Steps:
1. User opens the app for the first time.
2. User selects the option to create an account.
3. User enters a valid email address.
4. User enters a password and confirms it.
5. User clicks the "Send Verification Link" button.
6. User checks the email inbox and clicks the verification link.
Expected Result: Account is successfully created in the database, and the user receives confirmation of registration.

Title: Account Creation with Existing Email
Preconditions: User has installed the app on their device.
Test Data: Email address already registered in the system
Test Steps:
1. User opens the app for the first time.
2. User selects the option to create an account.
3. User enters an email address already registered in the system.
4. User enters a password and confirms it.
5. User clicks the "Send Verification Link" button.
Expected Result: System displays an error message indicating the email is already registered.

Title: Successful Login with Valid Credentials
Preconditions: User has installed the app and created an account.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the app.
2. User enters a valid email address.
3. User enters the correct password.
4. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result: User is successfully authenticated and gains access to the application.

Title: Failed Login with Invalid Credentials
Preconditions: User has installed the app.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the app.
2. User enters a valid email address.
3. User enters an incorrect password.
4. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result: System displays an error message indicating invalid credentials.

Title: Modify Class Schedule
Preconditions: User has logged in and is on the calendar page.
Test Data: List of classes to remove, new classes to add
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the "modificare l’orario" button.
2. User removes unwanted classes from the list.
3. User adds new classes to the list.
4. User clicks the "conferma" button.
Expected Result: Changes are saved, and the updated schedule is displayed.

Title: Cancel Modifications to Class Schedule
Preconditions: User has logged in and is on the calendar page.
Test Data: List of classes to remove, new classes to add
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the "modificare l’orario" button.
2. User removes unwanted classes from the list.
3. User adds new classes to the list.
4. User clicks the "annulla" button.
Expected Result: No changes are saved, and the original schedule is displayed.

Title: Create a New Signal Report
Preconditions: User has logged in and is on the signal reports page.
Test Data: Problem description, specific details, report type
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the button to create a new signal report.
2. User selects the report type.
3. User describes the problem and enters specific details.
4. User clicks the "conferma e invia" button.
Expected Result: The new signal report is saved in the database.

Title: Visualize User Profile Data
Preconditions: User must be logged in to their account.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the main screen.
2. User selects the "Visualizzazione Profilo" (Profile View) option.
Expected Result: The user’s profile page displays all previously inserted data (e.g., workplace/study location).

Title: Modify Profile Settings Without Changes
Preconditions: User must be logged in and on the "Visualizzazione Profilo" page.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the "modifica" (edit) button in the profile section.
2. User reviews the pre-filled form with current data.
3. User clicks the "conferma" (confirm) button without making any changes.
Expected Result: The system saves the existing data unchanged, and the user remains on the profile page.

Title: Cancel Profile Modifications
Preconditions: User must be logged in and on the "Visualizzazione Profilo" page.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the "modifica" (edit) button in the profile section.
2. User alters one or more fields in the form.
3. User clicks the "annulla" (cancel) button.
Expected Result: All changes are discarded, and the user returns to the profile page with original data intact.

Title: Remove and Replace User Seat/Workplace
Preconditions: User must be logged in and on the "Visualizzazione Profilo" page with an existing seat/workplace.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the "modifica" (edit) button in the seat/workplace section.
2. User selects the option to remove the current seat/workplace.
3. User selects a new seat/workplace from the available options.
4. User clicks the "conferma" (confirm) button.
Expected Result: The system updates the user’s seat/workplace to the newly selected option and saves the change permanently.

Title: Logout from Application
Preconditions: User must be logged in and on the "Visualizzazione Profilo" page.
Test Steps: 1. User clicks the logout button in the profile section.
Expected Result: The user is redirected to the login screen, and the authentication token is invalidated and removed from the database.

Title: Display Transportation Schedule Change Notification
Preconditions: User must be logged in.
Test Data: Simulated delay or schedule change for a user-relevant transportation mean (e.g., train/bus).
Test Steps: 1. System detects a change in the schedule of a transportation mean linked to the user’s profile (e.g., delay or cancellation).
Expected Result: A notification appears on-screen informing the user about the change, specifying the affected transportation mean and its impact on the user’s route.

Title: Create New User Account
Preconditions: None
Test Data: Valid email address, unique username, and password meeting complexity requirements.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the account creation screen.
2. User enters email, username, and password in the required fields.
3. User clicks the "Crea Account" (Create Account) button.
Expected Result: The system validates the input, creates the account, and redirects the user to the login screen. The user’s data is stored for future sessions.

Title: Login with Valid Credentials
Preconditions: User account must exist in the system.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the login screen.
2. User enters registered email/username and password.
3. User clicks the "Accedi" (Login) button.
Expected Result: The system authenticates the user, generates a valid token, and redirects the user to the main screen with access to their profile and other functionalities.

Title: Calculate Travel Time to Train Station
Preconditions: User is logged in and has not yet selected a train for a specific trip.
Test Data: User’s current location, destination station, desired departure time.
Test Steps:
1. User inputs starting location and destination station.
2. User selects a desired departure time.
3. System calculates the time required to reach the station from the user’s current location.
Expected Result: The application displays the estimated time needed to reach the station, allowing the user to make an informed decision.

Title: Select and Save Preferred Trains for Daily Commute
Preconditions: User has logged in and has defined work/study schedule.
Test Data: User’s work/study schedule (days, times), list of available trains.
Test Steps:
1. User selects a day of the week (e.g., Monday).
2. System displays available trains matching the user’s schedule.
3. User selects one or more trains for departure and arrival.
4. User confirms the selection.
Expected Result: The selected trains are saved in the user’s profile, and future notifications will only alert about changes to these trains.

Title: View Saved Train and Work/School Schedule
Preconditions: User has previously selected trains and defined work/school schedule.
Test Data: User’s profile data (saved trains, work/school timings).
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Visualizzazione profilo" section.
2. User requests to view saved data.
Expected Result: The application displays the user’s saved trains and work/school schedule accurately.

Title: Display Detailed Information for Selected Train
Preconditions: User has selected a train for a trip.
Test Data: Train ID, type, departure station, arrival station, intermediate stations, scheduled times, potential delays.
Test Steps:
1. User selects a train from their saved list or search results.
2. User requests detailed information about the selected train.
Expected Result: The application displays train ID, type, departure/arrival stations (including intermediates), scheduled times, and any known delays.

Title: Create and Save a Transport Issue Report
Preconditions: User is logged in and encounters a transport issue (e.g., delay, cancellation).
Test Data: Description of the issue, affected train/station, timestamp.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Creazione segnalazione" section.
2. User fills in the details of the transport issue (e.g., "Train X delayed by 30 minutes at Station Y").
3. User submits the report.
Expected Result: The report is created and saved in the database.

Title: View Transport Issue Reports
Preconditions: One or more transport issue reports exist in the system.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Visualizzazione segnalazione" section.
2. User requests to view all reports.
Expected Result: The application displays all saved transport issue reports.

Title: Provide Optimized Solutions for Ad-Hoc Trips
Preconditions: User has not predefined a trip but needs to travel to a destination.
Test Data: Departure station, arrival station, desired departure time.
Test Steps:
1. User inputs departure station, arrival station, and desired departure time.
2. User requests trip solutions.
Expected Result:
The application provides a list of optimized solutions with:
Orario di partenza (departure time) ≥ user’s desired time.
Orario di arrivo (arrival time) for each solution.
Codici dei treni consigliati (recommended train codes).

Title: Notify User of Preferred Train Changes
Preconditions: User has saved preferred trains and the system detects a change (e.g., delay, cancellation).
Test Data: Updated train schedule for a saved train.
Test Steps:
1. System detects a change in a user’s saved train (e.g., delay).
2. User is notified via the application.
Expected Result: The user receives a notification about the variation in their preferred train, including details of the change.

Title: Access, Modify, or Insert Lesson Schedule
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the lesson schedule page.
2. User selects "Modifica" (edit) or "Inserisci" (insert) option.
3. User updates or adds lesson timings.
4. User confirms changes.
Expected Result: The updated lesson schedule is saved, and the application reflects these changes in future train recommendations.

Title: View Lesson Schedule
Preconditions: User has defined a lesson schedule.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Visualizzazione orario" section.
2. User requests to view lesson schedule.
Expected Result: The application displays the user’s lesson schedule accurately.

Title: Insert/Modify Work/Study Location
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Data: Work/study location details (e.g., station name, address).
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the profile customization section.
2. User selects "Inserisci" (insert) or "Modifica" (modify) for work/study location.
3. User inputs or updates location details.
4. User confirms changes.
Expected Result: The work/study location is saved in the database, and the application uses this data for future travel calculations.

Title: Display Available Trains Based on User Input
Preconditions: User has defined work/study schedule and location.
Test Data: User’s schedule, location, and current date/time.
Test Steps:
1. User requests to view available trains for a specific trip (e.g., morning commute).
2. System filters trains based on user’s predefined data.
Expected Result: The application displays a list of available trains matching the user’s criteria, allowing selection.

Title: Access Report Page
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Steps: 1. User navigates to the "Accesso pagina segnalazioni" section.
Expected Result: The application loads the report page, allowing the user to view or create new reports.

Title: Notify User of Train Variations
Preconditions: System detects a variation in a user’s preferred or scheduled train.
Test Steps:
1. System detects a variation in a train the user relies on.
2. User is notified via the application.
Expected Result: The user is informed about the variation, and the application provides updated options if available.

Title: View Train Variations
Preconditions: One or more train variations exist in the system.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Visualizzazione variazioni mezzi" section.
2. User requests to view variations.
Expected Result: The application displays all current train variations, including cancellations or delays.

Title: Verify intuitive UI after 30-minute training
Preconditions: Application installed on a test device
Test Steps:
1. Train a test user for 30 minutes on basic application features.
2. Ask the user to perform 5 common tasks (e.g., login, route search, settings update) without guidance.
3. Observe if the user completes tasks without errors.
Expected Result: User completes all tasks successfully without errors.

Title: Check absence of flashing animations
Preconditions: Application launched on a test device
Test Steps:
1. Navigate through all application screens (e.g., login, home, settings).
2. Observe UI elements for any animations with flashing lights.
Expected Result: No flashing animations are present in any screen.

Title: Verify help page accessibility
Preconditions: Application installed on a test device
Test Steps:
1. Open the help page from the main menu.
2. Verify the presence of instructions for all listed functional requirements (RF-01 to RF-20).
Expected Result: Help page contains clear instructions for all referenced functionalities.

Title: Check application storage size
Preconditions: Application installed on a test device
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on a test device.
2. Measure the occupied storage space using device settings.
Expected Result: Application occupies ≤100 MB of storage.

Title: Verify route calculation response time
Preconditions: Application installed on a test device with internet connection
Test Steps:
1. Open the route search feature.
2. Enter the two predefined locations.
3. Start the route calculation timer.
4. Observe the time taken to display the best route.
Expected Result: Route calculation completes within 10 seconds.

Title: Verify screen transition time
Preconditions: Application installed on a test device
Test Steps:
1. Navigate from the home screen to any other screen (e.g., settings).
2. Start a timer immediately after selecting the transition.
3. Stop the timer when the new screen fully loads.
Expected Result: Screen transition completes within 1 second.

Title: Verify crash frequency
Preconditions: Application installed on a test device
Test Steps:
1. Use the application intensively for 30 days (e.g., daily login, route searches, settings changes).
2. Monitor for crashes using device logs or error reports.
Expected Result: No more than 1-2 crashes occur in the 30-day period.

Title: Verify correct installation
Preconditions: None
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on a clean test device.
2. Verify the application icon appears on the home screen.
3. Open the application and confirm all core features (e.g., login screen) are accessible.
Expected Result: Installation completes successfully without errors.

Title: Verify data removal on disinstallation
Preconditions: Application installed and used on a test device with test data
Test Data: Sample user data (e.g., saved routes, preferences)
Test Steps:
1. Create and save test data within the application.
2. Disinstall the application via device settings.
3. Verify no residual data remains (e.g., check storage for leftover files).
Expected Result: All user data is removed upon disinstallation.

Title: Verify Android 7.0+ compatibility
Preconditions: None
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on a device running Android 7.0 (Nougat).
2. Perform basic operations (e.g., login, navigation).
Expected Result: Application functions correctly without errors.

Title: Verify cross-device data sync
Preconditions: Two test devices (Device A and Device B) with the same user account
Test Data: Sample user data (e.g., saved preferences, routes)
Test Steps:
1. Save test data on Device A.
2. Log in to the same account on Device B.
3. Verify saved data appears on Device B.
Expected Result: All user data and settings sync correctly across devices.

Title: Firebase Database Integration
Preconditions: Android development environment with Firebase SDK configured
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on an Android device.
2. Attempt to store a sample user profile (e.g., name, email) in the app.
3. Verify data persistence across app restarts.
Expected Result: Data is successfully stored and retrieved using Firebase without errors.

Title: User Data Privacy Compliance
Preconditions: User account created with personal data (e.g., name, email, phone)
Test Data: Sensitive user data (e.g., address, payment details)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to settings and attempt to export user data.
2. Verify the exported data excludes unnecessary fields (e.g., payment details).
3. Attempt to delete user account via app settings.
Expected Result: Only required data (e.g., name, email) is exported; account deletion removes all user data from the device.

Title: Minimal Data Exposure to Trenitalia API
Preconditions: Internet connection active; user logged in
Test Data: Valid train search query (e.g., "Milan to Rome, 10:00 AM")
Test Steps:
1. Enter search criteria (departure station, arrival station, minimum departure time).
2. Submit the query to the app.
3. Intercept API call using a tool (e.g., Charles Proxy) to verify transmitted data.
Expected Result: Only departure station, arrival station, and minimum departure time are sent to the API; no additional user data is exposed.

Title: Offline Functionality Check
Preconditions: App installed; user logged in
Test Steps:
1. Disable internet connection on the device.
2. Attempt to perform a train search.
3. Attempt to access user profile.
Expected Result: App displays an error message stating "Internet connection required" and prevents further actions until connectivity is restored.

Title: Response Time Validation
Preconditions: Stable internet connection; device on Wi-Fi
Test Steps:
1. Start a timer before submitting the search query.
2. Submit the query and record the time taken to display results.
3. Repeat the test 5 times with different queries.
Expected Result: All responses are received within 10 seconds.

Title: App Storage Usage
Preconditions: App installed on a clean device
Test Steps:
1. Install the app on the device.
2. Check the app’s storage usage via device settings.
3. Perform basic actions (e.g., login, search, save a route).
4. Recheck storage usage.
Expected Result: Total app storage remains under 100 MB before and after usage.

Title: User Training Validation
Preconditions: Fresh Android device with no prior app usage
Test Steps:
1. Provide a 30-minute guided tutorial on app basics (login, search, bookmarking).
2. Ask the user to perform 3 independent tasks (e.g., search a route, save a favorite, log out).
Expected Result: User completes all tasks without errors or assistance.

Title: Accessibility Compliance (No Flashing Lights)
Preconditions: App installed; device in dark mode
Test Steps:
1. Navigate through all app screens (login, search, results, profile).
2. Observe animations and transitions for flashing/rapidly changing light patterns.
Expected Result: No animations with flashing lights are present in any screen.

Title: Help Page Functionality
Preconditions: App installed; user logged in
Test Steps:
1. Access the help page from the main menu.
2. Attempt to resolve a common issue (e.g., "How to book a ticket?").
3. Follow the help instructions to perform the action.
Expected Result: Help page provides clear steps; user successfully completes the action.

Title: Crash-Free Operation
Preconditions: App installed; user logged in
Test Steps:
1. Perform 10 random actions (e.g., search, filter, save route, logout).
2. Monitor for crashes or forced closes.
Expected Result: No crashes or forced closes occur during the test.

Title: Data Removal on Uninstall
Preconditions: User data created (e.g., saved routes, profile)
Test Steps:
1. Uninstall the app via device settings.
2. Check for residual app data (e.g., cached files, databases).
Expected Result: All user data is permanently removed from the device.

Title: Error Handling for Invalid Input
Preconditions: App installed; user logged in
Test Data: Invalid search criteria (e.g., empty fields, special characters)
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to submit a train search with invalid input (e.g., no departure station).
2. Attempt to enter non-alphabetic characters in the "name" field during registration.
Expected Result: App displays clear error messages; no crashes or data corruption occurs.

Title: Android Version Compatibility
Preconditions: Android emulator configured with Android 7.0 (Nougat)
Test Steps:
1. Install the app on an Android 7.0 emulator.
2. Perform basic actions (e.g., login, search, logout).
Expected Result: App installs and functions without errors on Android 7.0.

Title: Cross-Device Data Sync
Preconditions: User account created on Device A; data saved (e.g., saved routes)
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the same account on Device B.
2. Verify saved routes and settings are present.
Expected Result: All user data and settings are synced and accessible on Device B.

Title: User Registration on First Launch
Preconditions: User has installed the application.
Test Data: Valid email, username, password, and required registration fields.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application for the first time.
2. User clicks the "Registrati" (Register) button.
3. User fills in the registration form with valid credentials.
4. User submits the form.
Expected Result: User registration is successful, and the user is redirected to the login screen or main page.

Title: User Login with Valid Credentials
Preconditions: User is registered and credentials are stored in the database.
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application.
2. User enters valid username and password.
3. User clicks the "Accedi" (Login) button.
Expected Result: User is redirected to the main page of the application.

Title: User Logout
Preconditions: User is authenticated and logged in.
Test Steps: 1. User clicks the "Esci" (Logout) button.
Expected Result: User is redirected to the login page, and the token file is deleted.

Title: View User Profile Information
Preconditions: User has logged in to the application.
Test Steps: 1. User navigates to the profile section.
Expected Result: User sees all current profile information, including work/study location.

Title: Edit User Profile Data
Preconditions: User is on the profile page.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the "modifica" (Edit) button.
2. User modifies the form fields with new data.
3. User submits the changes.
Expected Result: The profile is updated with the new information.

Title: Register and Modify Work/School Hours
Preconditions: User has logged in.
Test Steps:
1. User enters work/school hours in the designated fields.
2. User submits the form.
3. User modifies the hours and submits again.
Expected Result: The application displays recommended transportation options based on the updated hours, and the hours are saved in the database.

Title: Display Train Suggestions Based on Schedule
Preconditions: User has previously entered work/school hours.
Test Steps: 1. User opens the calendar section.
Expected Result: The application displays possible train solutions for the user’s schedule.

Title: Select Preferred Trains from Suggestions
Preconditions: User has logged in and entered work/school hours.
Test Steps:
1. User adds or modifies their schedule.
2. User views the list of suggested trains.
3. User selects one or more trains as preferred.
Expected Result: The application confirms the selection and updates the user’s preferred trains.

Title: View User’s Train and Calendar Data
Preconditions: User has accessed the relevant page.
Test Steps: 1. User navigates to the page showing train and calendar data.
Expected Result: User sees all relevant information about selected trains and their schedule.

Title: Display Train Information and Delays
Preconditions: User is on the calendar page.
Test Steps: 1. User accesses the calendar section.
Expected Result: User sees details of recommended trains, including train ID, departure/arrival stations, intermediate stops, and potential delays.

Title: Create a Transportation Issue Report
Preconditions: User is on the reporting page.
Test Data: Valid report details (e.g., issue description, location, date).
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the "Crea segnalazione" (Create Report) button.
2. User fills in the report form with details.
3. User submits the form.
Expected Result: The report is created and displayed to the user, and the new report appears in the list of reports.

Title: View Transportation Issue Reports
Preconditions: User is authenticated and logged in.
Test Steps: 1. User clicks the button to access the reporting page.
Expected Result: The application displays a list of existing transportation issue reports.

Title: Search for trains based on departure/arrival stations and time
Preconditions: User must be logged in
Test Data: Departure station: "Milan Central", Arrival station: "Rome Termini", Departure time: "14:30"
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the train search page.
2. User enters departure station "Milan Central".
3. User enters arrival station "Rome Termini".
4. User enters departure time "14:30".
5. User submits the search.
Expected Result: System displays a list of trains matching the specified departure/arrival stations and time.

Title: Save selected train solution to user profile
Preconditions: User is logged in and has searched for trains
Test Data: User selects a train from the search results
Test Steps:
1. User searches for trains (departure: "Milan Central", arrival: "Rome Termini", time: "14:30").
2. System displays a list of trains.
3. User selects a train from the list.
Expected Result: Selected train solution is saved in the user’s local profile and database.

Title: Access variations page
Preconditions: User has added at least one train to favorites or scheduled for today
Test Steps: 1. User clicks on the "variazioni" (variations) button.
Expected Result: System displays the variations page showing all changes to preferred/scheduled trains.

Title: Display train variations for preferred/scheduled trains
Preconditions: User has added trains to favorites or scheduled for today, and at least one train has changes (e.g., delay, cancellation)
Test Steps: 1. User navigates to the variations page.
Expected Result: System displays information about changes (e.g., delays, cancellations) for the user’s preferred/scheduled trains.

Title: Access calendar page to view/modify personal schedule
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Steps: 1. User clicks on the "calendario" (calendar) button.
Expected Result: System displays the calendar page showing the user’s work/study schedule.

Title: View personal work/study schedule
Preconditions: User is authenticated
Test Steps: 1. User navigates to the schedule section.
Expected Result: System displays the user’s work/study schedule.

Title: Insert and modify user’s seat location
Preconditions: User is on the profile visualization page
Test Steps:
1. User clicks to modify seat location.
2. User enters "Building A, Floor 3" as the new seat location.
3. User submits the change.
Expected Result: System displays the updated seat location and saves it to the database.

Title: Verify seat location update in database
Preconditions: User has modified seat location
Test Steps:
1. User logs out and logs back in.
2. User navigates to the profile page.
Expected Result: System displays the updated seat location ("Building A, Floor 3") from the database.

Title: Display compatible trains based on schedule and seat location
Preconditions: User has inserted work/study schedule and seat location
Test Data: Work schedule: "09:00-17:00", Seat location: "Building A, Floor 3"
Test Steps: 1. User navigates to the train list section.
Expected Result: System displays a list of trains compatible with the user’s schedule and seat location.

Title: Select a train from the compatible list
Preconditions: User has viewed the list of compatible trains
Test Data: User selects "Treno Regionale 12345" from the list
Test Steps:
1. User views the list of compatible trains.
2. User selects "Treno Regionale 12345".
Expected Result: System allows the user to proceed with the selected train (no explicit output specified, but selection is implied).

Title: Access notifications page
Preconditions: User is authenticated
Test Steps: 1. User clicks on the button to access the notifications page.
Expected Result: System displays the list of all notifications.

Title: Display notifications for train changes
Preconditions: User has added trains to favorites or scheduled for today, and at least one train has changes
Test Steps: 1. User navigates to the notifications page.
Expected Result: System displays a notification about changes (e.g., delays, cancellations) for the user’s preferred/scheduled trains.

Title: Receive push notification for train changes
Preconditions: User has added trains to favorites or scheduled for today, and a train has changes (e.g., delay)
Test Steps: 1. System detects a change (e.g., delay) for a user’s preferred/scheduled train.
Expected Result: User receives a push notification with details about the change.

Title: Verify Firebase Database Integration for User Data Storage
Preconditions: Firebase account and database instance are set up for the project.
Test Data: Sample user account data (e.g., username, email, password hash).
Test Steps:
1. Create a new user account in the application.
2. Submit user data (e.g., username, email, password) for registration.
3. Verify the data is sent to Firebase.
4. Retrieve the stored data from Firebase and validate its integrity.
Expected Result: User data is successfully stored in Firebase and retrievable without corruption.

Title: Verify Data Modification in Firebase
Preconditions: A user account exists in Firebase with sample data.
Test Data: Updated user profile data (e.g., new email, password change).
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application using existing credentials.
2. Modify user profile data (e.g., change email or password).
3. Submit the changes to the application.
4. Retrieve the updated data from Firebase.
Expected Result: Modified user data is correctly updated in Firebase.

Title: Verify Data Deletion in Firebase
Preconditions: A user account exists in Firebase.
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application using the account to be deleted.
2. Initiate account deletion from the application.
3. Confirm deletion via Firebase.
4. Attempt to log in with the deleted account credentials.
Expected Result: Account data is permanently deleted from Firebase, and login with deleted credentials fails.

Title: Verify Application Installation on Minimum Android Version
Preconditions: Android emulator or device with Android 7.0 (Nougat) installed.
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on an Android 7.0 device/emulator.
2. Launch the application.
3. Navigate through at least one primary function (e.g., login screen).
Expected Result: Application installs successfully and runs without crashes on Android 7.0.

Title: Verify Application Performance with Minimum RAM
Preconditions: Android device/emulator with exactly 1 GB RAM.
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on a device with 1 GB RAM.
2. Open the application and perform a series of actions (e.g., login, navigate to settings).
3. Monitor system performance (e.g., no lag, no forced closes).
Expected Result: Application runs fluently without performance degradation or crashes.

Title: Verify Application Installation with Minimum Storage
Preconditions: Android device/emulator with exactly 100 MB free storage.
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to install the application on a device with 100 MB free storage.
2. Verify installation completes without errors.
Expected Result: Application installs successfully without storage-related errors.
